text: Improvements in technology for producing all goods must result in

options.A: an inward shift in the production possibilities curve
options.B: an outward shift in the production possibilities curve
options.C: a flatter production possibilities curve
options.D: a steeper production possibilities curve
options.E: greater unemployment of labor

text: The quantity of peanuts supplied increased from 40 tons per week to 60 tons per week when the price of peanuts increased from $4 per ton to $5 per ton. The price elasticity of supply for peanuts over this price range is

options.A: elastic
options.B: inelastic
options.C: unit elastic
options.D: perfectly elastic
options.E: perfectly inelastic

text: Which of the following best describes the law of demand?

options.A: The price of a good increases when the demand for the good increases.
options.B: The price of a good decreases when the supply of the good decreases.
options.C: When the price of a good increases, its demand decreases.
options.D: When the price of a good decreases, its quantity demanded increases.
options.E: Demand creates its own supply.

text: Assume that ice cream is a normal good. If the price of ice cream decreases, the substitution effect and the income effect will lead to which of the following changes in ice cream consumption? Substitution Effect Income Effect

options.A: Increase / Decrease
options.B: Increase / Increase
options.C: Increase / No change
options.D: Decrease / Increase
options.E: Decrease / No change

text: Short-run marginal costs eventually increase because of the effects of

options.A: increasing marginal product
options.B: diminishing marginal product
options.C: diseconomies of scale
options.D: economies of scale
options.E: increasing fixed costs

text: If a government eliminated an effective price floor in a market, all of the following would occur EXCEPT:

options.A: The surplus would be eliminated.
options.B: The price would decrease.
options.C: The quantity supplied would decrease.
options.D: The quantity demanded would increase.
options.E: The supply of the good would increase.

text: Which of the following must be true if a firm is experiencing economies of scale? any part of this page is illegal.

options.A: All costs are explicit.
options.B: Long-run average total cost decreases as the firm's output increases.
options.C: Economic profits decrease as the firm's output increases.
options.D: Long-run average total cost remains constant as the firm's output decreases.
options.E: Proportionate increases in inputs result in less-than-proportionate increases in output.

text: Compared to a perfectly competitive industry with the same demand and cost curves, a monopoly's price and output will be which of the following? Price Output

options.A: The same Higher
options.B: Higher The same
options.C: Higher Lower
options.D: Lower The same
options.E: Lower Higher

text: If the demand for a good is perfectly price inelastic in the short run and the supply curve is upward sloping, imposing a sales tax on the good will

options.A: leave the price paid by consumers unchanged
options.B: decrease the after-tax revenues received by suppliers
options.C: increase the after-tax revenues received by suppliers
options.D: not change the after-tax revenues received by suppliers
options.E: not change the total expenditures by consumers on the good

text: The characteristic of oligopolistic firms that makes them different from all other types of firms is that oligopolistic firms

options.A: are regulated by a state agency or federal agency
options.B: consider each other's decisions
options.C: advertise their products
options.D: produce differentiated products
options.E: produce identical products

text: For the firm shown in the graph above, the short- run, profit-maximizing strategy would be to set output at

options.A: Q1, price at P1, and suffer a loss
options.B: Q1, price at P3, and earn an economic profit
options.C: Q1, price at P3, and earn only a normal profit
options.D: Q2, price at P2, and earn an economic profit
options.E: Q2, price at P2, and earn only a normal profit

text: Which of the following will tend to make the demand for a product more elastic?

options.A: New firms which produce similar products enter the industry.
options.B: A change in taste and preferences makes the product more desirable.
options.C: The product is necessary for use with a complement.
options.D: Production of the product is protected by a patent.
options.E: Production cost of the product decreases.

text: In a perfectly competitive labor market, an increase in an effective minimum wage will result in

options.A: an increase in the supply of workers
options.B: a decrease in the supply of workers
options.C: a decrease in the demand for workers
options.D: more workers being hired
options.E: fewer workers being hired

text: Which of the following taxes contributes most to decreasing inequality in the distribution of income? any part of this page is illegal.

options.A: Progressive income taxes
options.B: Sales taxes
options.C: Proportional income taxes
options.D: Excise taxes
options.E: Import tariffs on necessities

text: A free-rider problem arises when a good is

options.A: nonrival
options.B: nondepletable
options.C: nonexcludable
options.D: produced in a competitive market
options.E: produced in a monopolistic market

text: According to the production possibilities curve above, which of the following is true?

options.A: The opportunity cost of producing another unit of good Y in terms of good X increases as more of good Y is produced.
options.B: The opportunity cost of producing another unit of good Y in terms of good X decreases as more of good Y is produced.
options.C: The opportunity cost of producing another unit of good X in terms of good Y increases as more of good X is produced.
options.D: The opportunity cost of producing another unit of good X in terms of good Y decreases as more of good X is produced.
options.E: The opportunity cost of producing another unit of either good remains constant as the production of the other good increases.

text: Which of the following is true in a capitalist economy?

options.A: The factors of production are collectively owned.
options.B: The factors of production are distributed according to needs.
options.C: The prices of goods and services are usually determined by the government.
options.D: The prices of goods and services are set so that an equitable distribution of private property is achieved.
options.E: Prices serve as incentives for factors of production to move to the markets where they are most valued.

text: A city transit authority increases the price of sub- way and bus tickets from $1.25 to $1.50. If the demand for these tickets is price inelastic, the number of people riding buses and subways and the city's revenues will most likely change in which of the following ways? Number of People Riding City's Revenues

options.A: Increase / Increase
options.B: Decrease / Increase
options.C: Decrease / Decrease
options.D: Decrease / Remain constant
options.E: Remain constant / Increase

text: The difference between what consumers are willing to pay for units of a good and the price consumers actually pay for units of the good is called

options.A: marginal utility
options.B: producer surplus
options.C: consumer surplus
options.D: economic rent
options.E: a positive externality any part of this page is illegal.

text: Which of the following is true if consuming one unit of a good yields 100 utils and consuming the second unit of the good increases satisfaction by 20 utils?

options.A: The marginal utility of the first unit is 20.
options.B: The marginal utility of the second unit is 80.
options.C: The marginal utility of the second unit is 120.
options.D: The total utility of consuming two units is 120.
options.E: The total utility of consuming one unit is greater than the total utility of consuming two units.

text: If a new tax on capital increases a firm's fixed cost of production, which of the following will occur in the short run?

options.A: Marginal cost will increase.
options.B: Average variable cost will increase.
options.C: Average total cost will increase.
options.D: The profit-maximizing level of output will increase.
options.E: The profit-maximizing level of output will decrease. • The marginal product of labor equals 250 units of output. • The marginal product of capital equals 750 units of output. • The price of labor is $50 per person. • The price of capital is $100 per unit.

text: Given the information above, which of the following is true for a firm buying its labor and capital inputs in a perfectly competitive market?

options.A: The firm is producing its current level of output with the least-cost combination of labor and capital.
options.B: The firm is maximizing profits with its current combination of inputs.
options.C: The firm's level of output will remain the same if 1 unit of capital is substituted for 2 units of labor.
options.D: The firm's input costs will decrease if 2 units of labor are substituted for 1 unit of capital.
options.E: The firm can reduce the cost of its current level of output by laying off workers and employing more capital.

text: At 100 units of output, a firm's total cost is $10,000. If the firm's total fixed cost is $4,000, its average variable cost is equal to
Which of the following statements correctly identifies a difference between perfect competition and monopolistic competition?

options.A: In perfect competition there are no barriers to entry, but there are strong barriers in monopolistic competition.
options.B: In perfect competition there are many firms, but in monopolistic competition there are only a few firms.
options.C: In perfect competition the firms all sell products that are exactly the same, but in monopolistic competition each firm sells a slightly differentiated product.
options.D: In perfect competition firms maximize profit by selling the quantity where marginal revenue equals marginal cost, but in monopolistic competition firms maximize profit by selling the quantity where marginal revenue exceeds marginal cost.
options.E: In perfect competition there are few consumers, but in monopolistic competition there are many consumers.

text: The most profitable level of output for any firm operating in the short run is the level of output at which

options.A: marginal revenue exceeds marginal cost by the highest amount
options.B: marginal revenue equals marginal cost
options.C: price exceeds average cost by the highest amount
options.D: price equals average cost
options.E: price equals marginal cost any part of this page is illegal.

text: For an unregulated monopolist, the profit- maximizing quantity will always be

options.A: in the elastic region of the demand curve
options.B: where marginal revenue equals price
options.C: where price equals average total cost
options.D: where price equals marginal cost
options.E: where the marginal cost curve intersects the demand curve

text: If a per-unit tax is imposed on a monopolist, how will the monopolist's marginal cost curve, output, and the price paid by consumers be affected? Marginal Cost Output Price

options.A: Shift down / Increase / Decrease
options.B: Shift down / Decrease / Decrease
options.C: No shift / Decrease / Decrease
options.D: Shift up / Decrease / Increase
options.E: Shift up / Increase / Increase

text: The demand curve for labor for a monopolist that faces a perfectly competitive factor market is called the

options.A: average product curve
options.B: marginal product curve
options.C: marginal revenue curve
options.D: marginal revenue product curve
options.E: value of the average product curve Number of Workers Parts per Week 0 0 1 100 2 190 3 270 4 340 5 400 6 450 7 480

text: The table above describes the production function of an auto parts manufacturer. Assume that the firm can hire as many workers as it wants at the market wage rate of $600 per week per worker and sell as many auto parts as it wants at the price of $10 per part. To maximize profits, the firm should hire

options.A: 0 workers
options.B: 1 worker
options.C: 3 workers
options.D: 5 workers
options.E: 7 workers

text: Monopolistically competitive firms are considered inefficient in allocating society's resources for which of the following reasons?

options.A: In equilibrium, the marginal benefit exceeds the price charged by the firms.
options.B: In equilibrium, the marginal benefit exceeds the marginal cost of production.
options.C: In equilibrium, the marginal revenue of the firm is not equal to its marginal cost.
options.D: In long-run equilibrium, the firm is earning economic profits.
options.E: Firms exhibit significant market power and therefore the number of firms in the industry is strictly limited. any part of this page is illegal.

text: As a factor of production, capital refers to the

options.A: money available to start a business
options.B: stocks and bonds issued by businesses to raise funds
options.C: financial investment of businesses
options.D: currency in circulation and deposits in financial institutions
options.E: tools and machinery used to produce goods and services

text: Which of the following will cause the supply curve for shoes to shift to the right?

options.A: An increase in the price of socks, assuming that shoes and socks are complements
options.B: A decrease in the price of sandals, assuming that shoes and sandals are substitutes
options.C: An increase in the wages of shoe workers
options.D: An increase in the number of firms producing shoes
options.E: A decrease in the income of consumers, assuming that shoes are normal goods

text: The diagram above depicts demand and supply curves in a city's rental housing market. If a price ceiling of $1,000 is imposed on the market, which of the following will occur?

options.A: There will be a surplus of rental housing in the city.
options.B: The demand curve for housing will shift to the right.
options.C: The supply curve for housing will shift to the right.
options.D: The quantity of rental housing supplied will decrease.
options.E: The quantity of rental housing demanded will increase.

text: For consumers, assume that fish sticks and tacos are substitutes. Which of the following graphs illustrates the effect on the taco market if the price of fish sticks increases?

options.A: See graph
options.B: See graph
options.C: See graph
options.D: See graph
options.E: See graph

text: In the short run, the firm will realize an economic loss but will continue to produce if the price is

options.A: below P1
options.B: between P1 and P2
options.C: below P2
options.D: between P2 and P3
options.E: between P3 and P4

text: Which of the following MUST be true in the long run?

options.A: The equilibrium price will be P2, since that is where marginal cost equals minimum average variable cost.
options.B: The equilibrium price will be above P3, since firms must make an economic profit to stay in business.
options.C: If the price is above P2, new firms will enter the industry.
options.D: If the price is above P3, new firms will enter the industry.
options.E: If the price is above P4, firms will exit the industry.

text: Which of the following MUST be true of the long run?

options.A: It is at least one year in duration.
options.B: All factors of production are variable.
options.C: At least one factor of production is fixed.
options.D: Marginal costs are constant.
options.E: Average total costs are constant.

text: In order for a firm to engage in price discrimi- nation, it must be

options.A: able to separate consumers into different groups based on demand elasticities
options.B: producing in the inelastic portion of its demand curve to raise its price and increase total revenue
options.C: a price taker
options.D: experiencing economies of scale in the relevant range of production
options.E: experiencing constant marginal cost

text: Which of the following market structures results in allocative efficiency?

options.A: Monopoly
options.B: Monopolistic competition
options.C: Perfect competition
options.D: Natural monopoly
options.E: Oligopoly any part of this page is illegal.

text: The following table shows the profits associated with the pricing strategies of two oligopolistic firms, Agronomia and Farmingdale. Each firm has two possible strategies: to charge a low price or a high price. The first entry in each cell shows the profits to Agronomia and the second the profits to Farmingdale.
Which of the following will occur in a perfectly competitive labor market if Firm X's demand for labor decreases? Equilibrium Market Wage Rate Employment by Firm X

options.A: Increase Increase
options.B: No change No change
options.C: No change Decrease
options.D: Decrease Decrease
options.E: Decrease No change

text: The graph above shows the marginal revenue product (MRP) and the market wage rate for a profit-maximizing firm. Which of the following is true of the firm's hiring of labor?

options.A: It should hire 15 workers.
options.B: It should hire between 15 and 40 workers.
options.C: It should hire 40 workers.
options.D: It should hire between 40 and 90 workers.
options.E: It should hire 90 workers.

text: Which of the following best defines a pure public good?

options.A: A good that is provided free of charge to the public
options.B: A good that is provided by the government, whether or not a fee is charged
options.C: A good that is efficiently produced only by a monopoly
options.D: A good whose benefits can be enjoyed by many consumers at the same time and from which consumers cannot be excluded
options.E: A good that by law must be available to all consumers without discrimination on the basis of sex, race, or religion

text: A perfectly competitive manufacturing industry pollutes public water in its production process, leaving the water unsuitable for use by the surrounding communities. At the market equilibrium output level, which of the following is true?

options.A: Marginal private cost exceeds marginal social cost.
options.B: Marginal private cost exceeds marginal private benefit.
options.C: Marginal social cost exceeds marginal social benefit.
options.D: The market equilibrium output is equal to the socially efficient output.
options.E: The market equilibrium output is less than the socially efficient output.

text: Assume that under new environmental regulations, tire companies have to pay taxes based on how much pollution is created when producing each tire. The price paid by consumers and the quantity of tires sold will most likely change in which of the following ways? Price Quantity

options.A: Increase / Decrease
options.B: Increase / Increase
options.C: Decrease / Increase
options.D: Decrease / Decrease
options.E: No change / No change

text: The market for goldfish is perfectly competitive. From year 1 to year 2, both the price and the quantity of goldfish sold increase. This is most likely caused by

options.A: an increase in the supply
options.B: a decrease in the supply
options.C: an increase in the demand
options.D: a decrease in the demand
options.E: a decrease in both the demand and the supply

text: Using the same amount of time and resources, Jack can assemble either 10 bikes or 5 computers, whereas Sam can assemble either 5 bikes or 5 computers. Based on the data, which of the following statements is correct?

options.A: Sam has an absolute advantage in assembling bikes.
options.B: Sam has an absolute advantage in assembling computers.
options.C: Sam has a comparative advantage in assembling bikes.
options.D: Jack has a comparative advantage in assembling bikes.
options.E: Jack has a comparative advantage in assembling both bikes and computers.

text: Given an increase in the price of material K- which is an input used to produce good X-and an increase in the price of good Y-which is a substitute for good X-which of the following will definitely occur?

options.A: The equilibrium price of good X will decrease.
options.B: The equilibrium price of good X will increase.
options.C: The equilibrium quantity of good X will be unaffected.
options.D: The equilibrium quantity of good X will increase.
options.E: The equilibrium quantity of good X will decrease.

text: An entrepreneur has earned enough total revenue to cover her accounting costs, but economic losses are being incurred. What must be true?

options.A: Her accounting costs are larger than her economic costs.
options.B: Her accounting profits are less than her implicit costs.
options.C: Her accounting profits are greater than her economic costs.
options.D: Her economic losses are less than her total fixed costs.
options.E: Her implicit costs are less than her accounting costs.

text: If nations specialize according to their comparative advantage and engage in international trade with each other, each nation can

options.A: produce outside its production possibilities curve
options.B: consume outside its production possibilities curve
options.C: shift its production possibilities curve to the right
options.D: become more self-sufficient
options.E: produce more of all goods

text: When total physical product is at its maximum, marginal physical product must be

options.A: greater than one
options.B: equal to one
options.C: equal to zero
options.D: less than zero
options.E: constant any part of this page is illegal.

text: Garcia is currently spending his entire lunch budget on 3 sodas and 4 hot dogs. At his current level of consumption, Garcia's marginal utility for sodas is 5 utils and his marginal utility for hot dogs is 10 utils. In order to maximize his total utility, Garcia should

options.A: consume more sodas and fewer hot dogs regardless of the prices
options.B: consume more hot dogs and fewer sodas regardless of the prices
options.C: maintain his current level of consumption of sodas and hot dogs regardless of the prices
options.D: maintain his current level of consumption if the price of a soda is $1 and the price of a hot dog is $2
options.E: maintain his current level of consumption if the price of a soda is $2 and the price of a hot dog is $1

text: As output of a firm increases, the difference between the firm's average total cost and its average variable cost gets smaller because the firm's

options.A: total cost is increasing
options.B: marginal cost is increasing
options.C: average fixed cost is decreasing
options.D: marginal product of labor is decreasing
options.E: long-run average total cost is decreasing

text: In a perfectly competitive market, a change in which of the following could cause a shift in the supply curve?

options.A: The incomes of consumers
options.B: The number of buyers
options.C: Technology
options.D: The price of the product
options.E: Tastes and preferences

text: Productive efficiency occurs when a firm produces output at a level at which

options.A: average total cost equals average revenue
options.B: average total cost is at a minimum
options.C: price exceeds average total cost
options.D: price equals marginal cost
options.E: marginal revenue exceeds average revenue

text: Which of the following is true of a monopolisti- cally competitive firm in long-run equilibrium?

options.A: Price equals marginal cost and average total cost.
options.B: Price equals average total cost but is greater than marginal cost.
options.C: Price equals marginal cost and is greater than average total cost.
options.D: The firm makes no attempt to differentiate its products.
options.E: The firm earns positive economic profits by producing at minimum average cost.

text: Assume that firms sell their output in a perfectly competitive product market and hire labor in a perfectly competitive labor market. If all other factors remain constant, an increase in the demand for the firms' product will result in which of the following changes in the labor market?

options.A: Firms will move down along the demand curve for labor and hire more workers.
options.B: The supply curve for labor will shift to the right.
options.C: The demand curve for labor will shift to the right.
options.D: The supply curve for labor will shift to the left.
options.E: The demand curve for labor will shift to the left.

text: A profit-maximizing firm will continue to hire workers until the

options.A: marginal factor cost of labor is equal to the marginal revenue product of labor
options.B: marginal factor cost of labor is equal to the price of the good produced using labor
options.C: marginal product of labor is equal to the marginal revenue product of labor
options.D: marginal product of labor is equal to the marginal factor cost of labor
options.E: marginal product of labor is equal to the price of labor any part of this page is illegal.

text: Assume that the marginal social benefit of the last unit of vaccination provided is greater than the marginal social cost. Which of the following can be used to achieve efficiency in the market for vaccination?

options.A: A per-unit subsidy for vaccinations
options.B: A lump-sum tax for vaccinations
options.C: A limit on the quantity of vaccinations
options.D: A price floor for vaccinations
options.E: A price ceiling for vaccinations

text: Which of the following government actions can correct the inefficiency caused by the existence of a monopoly?

options.A: Granting a per-unit subsidy on the monopolist's output
options.B: Granting an annual lump-sum subsidy to the monopolist
options.C: Imposing a per-unit tax on the monopolist's output
options.D: Imposing a price floor on the monopolist
